svg.text: Order #2406-089 — Table 4 — Closed May 27, 6:14 PM | Originally charged: $52.64 on Visa ••••4242
Tap items to include in refund
Keep this receipt for your records · Synalux POS v2.4
Options: Customer Dissatisfied / Wrong Item Delivered / Food Quality Issue / Billing Error ✓ / Order Never Arrived / Other
Audit log: staff_id + reason + Stripe refund_id → pos_audit_log (tamper-evident)
Deducted from today's gross in Z-report
Refund shown as deduction from gross sales
Cancels Stripe PaymentIntent before capture. Instant reversal, no bank delay.
pos_payments.status = voided · pos_audit_log entry created · No Stripe refund_id (never captured)
Calls stripe.refunds.create(payment_intent_id, amount_cents). Partial refund supported via amount param.
pos_refunds table: id, payment_id, amount_cents, reason, stripe_refund_id, status, staff_id, created_at
Both operations → pos_audit_log (staff_id, action, amount, reason, timestamp). Cash refund → pos_cash_events (type=paid_out).
Stripe webhook stripe.refund.updated → updates pos_refunds.status (pending→settled→failed). pos_payments.refund_amount tracks cumulative refunded.
Z-report: gross_sales − refunds = net_sales · refund_count · refund_total displayed separately for reconciliation
